summary: The July 4 scout found a sparse but useful Friday arXiv window for heavy-ion and physics-ML work. The strongest heavy-ion item is a compact quarkonium transport calculation testing excited-to-ground-state ratios as hot-medium probes across p-Pb and Pb-Pb. The most directly actionable ML item is a point-cloud classifier for charm- versus bottom-origin heavy-flavor electrons, with WavePID and DSGNAR offering credible transfer value for detector timing and physics-informed surrogate workflows. Several generic PDE/ML claims look interesting but should not be treated as validated for HEP or nuclear production tasks without domain-specific stress tests.
strongest_paper_summary: Probing hot QCD medium with heavy quarkonium in small and large collision systems is the strongest heavy-ion-relevant paper because it focuses on experimentally meaningful quarkonium yield ratios that suppress common cold-nuclear-matter uncertainties and tests one dynamical framework across p-Pb and Pb-Pb systems.

papers.title: Probing hot QCD medium with heavy quarkonium in small and large collision systems
papers.abstract: The paper uses a time-dependent Schroedinger-equation treatment of charmonium and bottomonium in complex in-medium potentials to describe excited-to-ground quarkonium yield ratios in p-Pb and Pb-Pb collisions.

papers.title: Determining the dynamic deformation of 140Ce by constraining coupled-channels parameters for fusion
papers.authors: Chandra Kumar, Rohan Biswas, J. Gehlot, Gonika, A. Parihari, N. Madhavan, A. Vinayak, Amritraj Mahato, S. Nath
papers.abstract: The paper combines new 16O+140Ce fusion data, literature 36S+140Ce data, Gaussian analytic-barrier modeling, chi-square minimization, Bayesian analysis, and coupled-channels tests to extract dynamic deformation parameters for 140Ce.

papers.title: Event-axis TMD measurements in e+e- and SIDIS
papers.authors: Daniel Diaz Fernandez, Patricia Andrea Gutierrez Garcia, Ignazio Scimemi, Wouter Waalewijn
papers.abstract: The paper develops operator-level and phenomenological ingredients for event-axis TMD fragmentation in e+e- and analogous 1-jettiness SIDIS measurements, with resummation and Pythia validation.

papers.title: Heavy-Flavor Electron Classification Using Hadronic Environment as Point Cloud
papers.authors: Jingyu Zhang, Wanbing He, Long Ma
papers.abstract: The paper represents the hadronic environment around heavy-flavor electrons as a point cloud and compares set-based neural architectures, including transformers, for charm-bottom electron separation.

papers.title: WavePID: Low-energy flavor identification using single-PMT time series in IceCube
papers.authors: IceCube Collaboration: R. Abbasi, M. Ackermann, J. Adams, J. A. Aguilar, M. Ahlers et al.
papers.abstract: WavePID is a template-based likelihood-ratio classifier that uses nanosecond-scale single-module timing observables to improve low-energy neutrino flavor identification when combined with graph-neural-network-selected samples.

papers.title: An Optimisation Framework for the Well-Conditioned Training of Physics-Informed Neural Networks
papers.authors: Joseph Webb, Sadok Jerad, Coralia Cartis
papers.abstract: The paper introduces a doubly-sketched Gauss-Newton optimizer with adaptive regularization and step control for PINNs, reporting major error and speed improvements across nonlinear, chaotic, multiscale, high-dimensional, and Navier-Stokes benchmarks.

papers.title: Beyond Adam: SOAP and Muon for Faster, Label-Efficient Training of Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
papers.authors: Gil Harari, Yoel Zimmermann, Ola Tangen Kulseng, Laura Zichi, Chuin Wei Tan, Marc L. Descoteaux, Boris Kozinsky
papers.abstract: The paper compares matrix-structured optimizers including SOAP, Muon, and SOAP-Muon for training NequIP and Allegro machine-learning interatomic potentials, finding faster convergence and better final accuracy than Adam, especially with partial force supervision.

papers.title: Frequency Shift Physics-Informed Extreme Learning Machine for Solving High-Frequency Partial Differential Equations
papers.authors: Xiong Xiong, Ruonan Zhai, Zheng Zeng, Sheng Zhou, Rongchun Hu, Zichen Deng
papers.abstract: The paper proposes frequency-shifted initialization for physics-informed extreme learning machines and reports large benchmark gains on several high-frequency PDE problems.
